  • 🇬🇧 English

Overlay bootstrap summary statistics onto a fit's $full grid

Source: R/bootstrap.R
dot-apply_bootstrap_overlay.Rd

The shared core of every bootstrap-CI path (.lossfit_bootstrap(), .premiumfit_bootstrap(), and the in-worker fit_sa() / fit_ratio() blocks). Given an already-resolved bootstrap result, it renames the bootstrap() summary columns to the caller's <role>_* schema, joins them onto full by (groups, cohort, dev), and – on projected cells only – overlays the bootstrap SE / CV (and quantile CI when present) over the analytical values.

Observed cells keep their analytical SE: the upper-triangle perturbation is a parameter-uncertainty tool, not a claim about observed-cell variability.

Usage

.apply_bootstrap_overlay(full, boots, role, groups, se_cols)

Arguments

full

The fit's $full data.table.

boots

A non-NULL resolved bootstrap result; boots$summary carries mean_proj, param_se, proc_se, total_se, total_cv, and optionally ci_lo / ci_hi.

role

Column-name prefix, "loss" or "premium".

groups

Group columns; the join key is c(groups, "cohort", "dev").

se_cols

Statistic suffixes to overlay – e.g. c("param_se", "proc_se", "total_se", "total_cv") for loss, c("total_se", "total_cv") for premium. The point projection is never overlaid; it stays analytical.

Value

full with the bootstrap values overlaid and the temporary _boot columns dropped.

On this page
